selected_goal: Make asset-reference validation fail when question-bank canonical image references are absent from the asset manifest.
why_this_now: The project objective prioritizes trustworthy canonical question and mark-scheme PNGs. The repo already has a reusable validate_asset_references gate, but it currently verifies that question-bank image paths resolve on disk without proving those same paths are indexed by asset_manifest.v1.json. A stale or incomplete manifest can therefore pass while downstream asset-id based consumers cannot reliably trace canonical images.
repo_evidence: .agent-loop/BACKLOG.md lists manifest/schema validation and missing/broken image-reference detection as preferred bounded improvements.
scripts/validate_asset_references.py delegates to exam_bank.asset_manifest.validate_asset_references and is listed in .agent-loop/project-gates.md.
src/exam_bank/asset_manifest.py validates missing question-bank image files and missing manifest files, but does not compare question-bank image fields against manifest canonical_path values.
tests/test_asset_manifest_storage_audit.py already contains fixture coverage for successful asset manifest generation and validation.
files_protected: input/** source PDFs and canonical raw inputs
acceptance_criteria: validate_asset_references reports a deterministic error when any non-empty question-bank image field references a path that exists on disk but is missing from asset_manifest.v1.json.
The error includes the question_id, source field, and missing path so the stale manifest can be repaired without visual guessing.
Existing valid manifest/question-bank fixtures still pass.
No generated output under output/, data/, or input/ is modified.
test_expectations: Add a fixture test that creates valid canonical question and mark-scheme PNG files, writes a deliberately incomplete asset manifest, and asserts validate_asset_references fails with missing_question_bank_manifest_path.
Run the targeted test file before implementation and expect the new test to fail.
Run the targeted test file after implementation and expect all tests in it to pass.
implementation_boundaries: Do not change extraction, rendering, mark-scheme mapping, image alignment, or export generation behavior.
Do not relax existing missing-file, duplicate-id, or export asset-id validation.
Only add the minimal manifest path comparison needed by validate_asset_references.
repo_hygiene_risk: The repo started dirty, including src/exam_bank/asset_manifest.py. This iteration is safe only if edits remain narrowly scoped and the final report distinguishes pre-existing changes from iteration changes.
rollback_plan: Remove the new test from tests/test_asset_manifest_storage_audit.py and remove the manifest-path comparison helper/call from src/exam_bank/asset_manifest.py.
done_when: The new missing-manifest-path test fails before implementation and passes after implementation.
The relevant project gate for asset-reference validation passes or any failure is explicitly audited.
git status --short and git diff --stat have been run after the audit.
